description: Grailguard Production Release — engine decomposition, data-driven JSON configs, TDD test coverage, rendering optimizations, new content, CI/CD, and GitHub Pages deployment

userStories.title: CI Pipeline Hardening
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CI pipeline to run lint, typecheck, and unit tests with coverage gates so that regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `biome ci .`, `tsc --noEmit`, `jest --ci --coverage`
CI fails if coverage drops below 80% on engine/ and db/ directories
Coverage report uploaded as artifact
E2E job runs Playwright against web export (continue-on-error until stable)
All steps use pinned action versions with SHA hashes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game Config — Wave & Pacing Parameters
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want all wave pacing parameters in a JSON file so that I can tune difficulty without touching code.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/waveConfig.json` with: waveBudgetBase (50), waveBudgetMultiplier (1.15), waveBudgetQuadratic (2), buildTimerBase (30), buildTimerLogCoeff (10), bossWaveInterval (5), victoryWave (20), spawnInterval (1.0), affixStartWave (6), affixChance (0.2), earlyStartBonusRate (2), waveCompletionBonusBase (50), waveCompletionBonusPerWave (10), interestRate (0.05), grailDamageNormal (1), grailDamageBoss (5)
Values match current hardcoded values exactly (regression-safe)
Tests written FIRST asserting config values produce identical behavior to hardcoded formulas
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Touches src/data/waveConfig.json (new), test file. Does NOT yet modify GameEngine.ts — that happens in US-020.

userStories.title: Game Config — Unit Stats
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want all unit stat blocks in a JSON file so that balance changes don't require TypeScript changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/unitConfig.json` with all fields from current UNITS record in constants.ts
Include per-wave scaling multiplier coefficient (0.15)
`constants.ts` imports and re-exports typed UNITS from JSON
Existing UnitConfig interface preserved as TypeScript overlay
Tests written FIRST verifying JSON-loaded stats match current hardcoded values
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game Config — Building Stats
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want all building configs in a JSON file so that costs, spawn times, and turret stats are data-driven.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/buildingConfig.json` with all fields from current BUILDINGS record in constants.ts
Include upgradeCostMultiplier (1.5) for exponential upgrade cost formula
`constants.ts` imports and re-exports typed BUILDINGS from JSON
Existing BuildingConfig interface preserved as TypeScript overlay
Tests written FIRST verifying JSON-loaded configs match current hardcoded values
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game Config — Spell Stats
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want spell costs, cooldowns, and effect values in a JSON file.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/spellConfig.json` with: spell IDs, faith costs, cooldowns, damage/heal values, AoE radii, durations, unlock costs for all 7 spells
All spell-related magic numbers removed from GameEngine.ts
Engine reads spell configs via static import
Tests written FIRST
Typecheck passes

Create `src/data/doctrineConfig.json` with node IDs, names, max levels, effects per level, costs
All relic/doctrine magic numbers removed from engine code
Tests written FIRST
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game Config — Combat Mechanics
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want combat formula coefficients in a JSON config.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/combatConfig.json` with: meleeSearchRange (8), rangedSearchRange (25), wallPriorityRange (5), poisonDamageRate (0.2), poisonDecayRate (2), freezeDecayRate (1), slowDuration (3), bossAoeRadius (8), vampiricHealPercent (0.5), armoredDamageReduction (0.5), swiftSpeedMultiplier (2), explosiveAoeDamage (50), explosiveAoeRadius (6), sellValuePercent (0.5)
Tests written FIRST
Typecheck passes

acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/renderConfig.json` with: camera position (0,100,70), zoom range (22-80), shake max offset (5), shake damping (0.9), shake lerp (0.1), particle size (0.3), particle gravity (30), particle drag (0.96), floating text rise speed (5), floating text lifetime (1), scenery density parameters

userStories.description: As a game designer, I want economy tunables (starting resources, cart speed, resource amounts) in JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/economyConfig.json` with: startingGold (300), startingWood (50), startingOre (0), startingGem (0), startingFaith (100), startingHealth (20), faithRegenRate (health/10 per sec), cartSpeed (5), cartSpeedWithRelic (10), woodDeliveryAmount (10), oreDeliveryAmount (1), gemDeliveryAmount (1), victoryCoinsMultiplier (25), defeatCoinsMultiplier (10)

userStories.title: Game Config — Map Generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want map generation parameters in a JSON config.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/mapConfig.json` with: mapSize (100), roadSampleCount (120), placementGridSize (5), wallMaxRoadDistance (4), spawnerMinRoadDistance (7), roadSplinePointCount (50), yukaPathPointCount (50), sceneryTreeCount, sceneryRockCount, scatterRadius

userStories.title: Seeded PRNG Module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a seeded pseudorandom number generator so that all randomness is deterministic and replayable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/systems/rng.ts` with: createRng(seed) returning { next(), nextInt(min,max), nextFloat(min,max), fork(label) }
Uses mulberry32 or xoshiro128 algorithm
Tests written FIRST in `src/__tests__/engine/systems/rng.test.ts`
Tests cover: deterministic sequences for same seed, different sequences for different seeds, fork independence
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Touches src/engine/systems/rng.ts (new), test file. Does NOT yet replace Math.random calls — that happens in subsystem modules.

userStories.title: DB Repository Test Suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for all database repository modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test files: profileRepo.test.ts, unlockRepo.test.ts, runRepo.test.ts, settingsRepo.test.ts, doctrineRepo.test.ts, codexRepo.test.ts, bootstrapRepo.test.ts
Tests use in-memory SQLite (:memory:) with schema migrations applied
Tests cover: CRUD operations, edge cases (duplicate inserts, missing records), transaction atomicity
profileRepo tests: coin balance updates, highest wave tracking, lifetime stats
runRepo tests: snapshot save/load, run history recording, active run lifecycle
unlockRepo tests: purchase transactions, unlock cost validation, spell vs building unlocks
>90% coverage on src/db/repos/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Touches src/__tests__/db/repos/*.test.ts (new). Independent of engine work.

userStories.title: Localization Support
userStories.description: As a non-English speaker, I want UI text to be translatable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/i18n/en.json` containing all UI strings (main menu, HUD, settings, codex, doctrine labels)
Create `src/i18n/index.ts` with t(key) translation function
All hardcoded UI strings in screens and HUD replaced with t() calls
Settings screen includes language selector (initially English only, structure ready for more)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Touches src/i18n/ (new), src/app/*.tsx, src/components/ui/HUD.tsx. Independent of engine work.

userStories.title: Haptic Feedback Integration
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want haptic feedback on key actions for satisfying tactile response.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/haptics.ts` with: impactLight(), impactMedium(), impactHeavy(), notificationSuccess(), notificationWarning()
Add expo-haptics as dependency in package.json
Haptics respect settings toggle (hapticsEnabled from settingsRepo)
No-op on web platform (Platform.OS check)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Touches src/engine/haptics.ts (new), package.json. Does NOT wire up to game events yet — that happens after audio bridge.

userStories.title: Runtime Telemetry Interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want runtime performance metrics exposed for debugging and optimization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/telemetry.ts` exporting GrailguardTelemetry interface with: fps, entityCount, activeParticles, activeProjectiles, activeUnits, memoryUsageMB, frameTimeMs
Telemetry singleton updated each frame in stepRunWorld()
Create `src/components/ui/DebugOverlay.tsx` toggled via settings
Telemetry disabled in production builds (tree-shaken via __DEV__ check)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audio Event Bus
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the simulation to emit audio events rather than calling SoundManager directly so that audio is decoupled from the engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/audio/audioBridge.ts` with typed event emitter
Event types: combat_hit, unit_death, building_placed, building_sold, wave_start, wave_complete, boss_spawn, spell_cast, game_over, ui_click
SoundManager.ts subscribes to bridge events instead of being called directly
All direct soundManager.* calls in GameEngine.ts replaced with event emissions
Engine has zero imports from SoundManager
Tests written FIRST (verify events emitted for each game action type)
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/engine.md

userStories.title: Extract Wave System Module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want wave spawning and pacing logic in its own module so it can be tested in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/systems/waveSystem.ts` with pure functions: calculateWaveBudget(wave), calculateBuildTimer(wave), allocateWaveBudget(budget, wave, rng), updateWaveState(dt, session, waveState), checkWaveCompletion(units)
All functions import from waveConfig.json
Functions are pure (no side effects, no global state mutation)
GameEngine.ts delegates to these functions
Tests written FIRST in `src/__tests__/engine/systems/waveSystem.test.ts`
Tests cover: budget formula accuracy, boss scheduling, affix assignment, wave completion detection
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/engine.md with new module
userStories.notes: Touches src/engine/systems/waveSystem.ts (new), src/engine/GameEngine.ts, test file, docs/architecture/engine.md

userStories.title: Extract Combat System Module

acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/systems/combatSystem.ts` with pure functions: findTarget(unit, enemies, allies), calculateDamage(attacker, defender), applyStatusEffects(unit, dt), processHealing(healer, allies), processBossAoe(boss, allies), processVampiricHeal(attacker, damage), processExplosiveDeath(unit, allies)
All functions import from combatConfig.json and unitConfig.json

GameEngine.ts delegates to these functions
Tests written FIRST in `src/__tests__/engine/systems/combatSystem.test.ts`
Tests cover: target selection priorities, damage with armor, vampiric healing, explosive death AoE, healer targeting, boss AoE radius
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slim GameEngine Orchestrator

userStories.title: Upgrade Cost Formula
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want building upgrade costs to follow an explicit exponential formula for balanced progression.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement in buildingSystem.ts: upgradeCost(baseCost, level) = baseCost * 1.5^(level-1)
Formula coefficient (1.5) loaded from buildingConfig.json upgradeCostMultiplier field
Applies to both gold and wood costs
Max level 5 enforced
Tests written FIRST verifying cost at each level (L1=1x, L2=1.5x, L3=2.25x, L4=3.375x, L5=5.0625x)
Typecheck passes
Update docs/game-design/buildings.md

userStories.title: InstancedMesh Particle System
userStories.description: As a developer, I want particles rendered via a pre-allocated InstancedMesh pool so that combat doesn't cause GC pressure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/components/3d/ParticlePool.tsx` with pre-allocated 500-slot InstancedMesh
Pool supports 4 types: hit sparks, death burst, heal glow, boss explosion
Particle bounce physics: on ground hit (y <= 0), velocity.y negated with 0.5x dampening
Replace current individual ParticleMesh.tsx spawning
Pool stats (active/total) readable for telemetry
Import render parameters from renderConfig.json
No visual regression — same particle colors, sizes, and behaviors
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/rendering.md

userStories.title: Reduced-FX Path
userStories.description: As a player on a low-end device, I want to disable particles and floating text so the game runs smoothly.
acceptanceCriteria: Read reducedFx setting from settings repo
When enabled: skip particle spawning, skip floating text, skip camera shake, skip world effects
No performance cost for checking the flag (early return in vfxSystem functions)
Settings screen toggle already exists — wire it to actual behavior in vfxSystem.ts
Tests written FIRST (verify VFX functions return early when reducedFx=true)
Typecheck passes

acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/engine/ai/playerGovernor.ts` using Yuka GOAP Think/Goal/GoalEvaluator
Implement goal evaluators: BuildStructureGoal, StartWaveGoal, RepairGoal, SmiteGoal
Each goal has calculateDesirability(session) scoring function
Governor emits WorldCommand objects (same interface as player input)
Create `src/data/governorConfig.json` with desirability weights and thresholds
GOAP planner selects highest-desirability action each tick
Tests written FIRST in `src/__tests__/engine/ai/playerGovernor.test.ts`
Tests cover: goal scoring under various game states, action selection priority
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/engine.md
userStories.notes: Touches src/engine/ai/playerGovernor.ts (new), src/data/governorConfig.json (new), test file, docs/

userStories.title: Day/Night Cycle
userStories.description: As a player, I want a day/night lighting cycle so that the game atmosphere changes over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/components/3d/DayNightCycle.tsx` component
Create `src/data/lightingConfig.json` with: time-of-day presets (dawn, day, dusk, night), transition durations, ambient/directional light colors and intensities per preset
Cycle advances per wave (day->dusk->night->dawn across 4 waves)
Smooth color/intensity interpolation between presets in useFrame
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/rendering.md

userStories.title: Elaborate Sanctuary Model
userStories.description: As a player, I want a visually impressive sanctuary with towers, rotating grail, and health-based degradation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/components/3d/Sanctuary.tsx` with: 4 corner towers with conical roofs, rotating grail at center (continuous Y rotation in useFrame), corner torches with flickering point lights
Health-based visual degradation: color shifts (lerp to red) and scale reduction as HP decreases
Procedural geometry (CylinderGeometry towers, SphereGeometry grail, ConeGeometry roofs)
Replaces current simple sanctuary mesh in Arena.tsx
Typecheck passes
Update docs/architecture/rendering.md

userStories.title: Biome System
userStories.description: As a player, I want multiple biome types that affect terrain visuals and gameplay modifiers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/data/biomeConfig.json` with: kings-road (default), desert-wastes (reduced faith regen, +gold from kills), frost-peaks (enemies start slowed, reduced build timer), shadow-marsh (enemies +20% HP, +rare drops)
Each biome defines: terrain color palette, ambient audio key, gameplay modifier multipliers, scenery type overrides
Biome selected at run start on main menu
Terrain colors and lighting reflect active biome
Gameplay modifiers applied in waveSystem.ts and combatSystem.ts
Tests written FIRST for gameplay modifiers
Typecheck passes
Update docs/game-design/combat.md and docs/architecture/rendering.md
userStories.notes: Touches src/data/biomeConfig.json (new), src/engine/systems/waveSystem.ts, src/engine/systems/combatSystem.ts, src/components/3d/Arena.tsx, src/app/index.tsx, test file, docs/

userStories.title: Leaderboard / Run History Screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my run history with stats so I can track improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/app/history.tsx` screen showing past runs
Display: date, waves survived, biome, difficulty, coins earned, total kills, duration
Sort by date (newest first)
Reads from runHistory table via runRepo
Accessible from main menu (add navigation button to index.tsx)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test Suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests that verify the game loads and core flows work in a real browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `e2e/gameFlow.spec.ts` covering: app loads, main menu renders, start run, build phase shows toychest, place a building, start wave, enemies spawn
Create `e2e/metaScreens.spec.ts` covering: navigate to codex/doctrine/settings screens, settings persist across reload
Tests run against `expo export -p web` static build
CI workflow runs Playwright against the export
Playwright config uses webServer to serve the export directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tutorial / Onboarding Flow
userStories.description: As a new player, I want a guided tutorial on my first run so that I understand building placement, wave starting, and resource management.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/components/ui/Tutorial.tsx` with step-by-step overlay
Tutorial steps: (1) Place a wall on the road, (2) Place a militia hut, (3) Start the wave, (4) Explain gold rewards, (5) Place a lumber camp and track
Tutorial state persisted in settings repo (tutorialComplete boolean)
Tutorial skippable at any point via Skip button
Highlights relevant UI elements with semi-transparent overlay and spotlight cutout
Typecheck passes
Update docs/memory-bank/productContext.md

userStories.title: Accessibility Improvements
userStories.description: As a player with disabilities, I want the UI to be accessible via screen readers and high contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: All TouchableOpacity elements have accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole props
High-contrast mode option in settings (increases text contrast, adds outlines to UI elements)
Screen reader announces: phase changes, wave number, gold balance, building placement success/failure via AccessibilityInfo.announceForAccessibility
Typecheck passes
